description: 'kirero' is the character or degree of a thing in relation to a stated purpose and criteria. A chair can be sturdy yet hard to repair, so several qualities may matter without becoming one score. The word evaluates the work or result, not the maker.
articulatory_notes: Release unvoiced 'k' from a velar closure into high-front unrounded 'i'. Give the tongue tip one quick contact at the alveolar ridge, or let it trill briefly, before stressed true-mid front 'e'. Repeat that contact and round the lips around final true-mid back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The two 'r' contacts turn through contrasting middle vowels, first front 'e' and then rounded back 'o'. The repeated motion suits a judgement made along more than one dimension.
usage_notes: The noun may name quality in general or one selected quality. 'kirema' (criterion) supplies the standard and 'kisome' (assess) the act of evaluating evidence against it. Fault, beauty, goodness, and compliance remain narrower claims.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Quality can keep a made thing's form and material distinct from its usefulness, repairability, or beauty.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Stated quality criteria keep evaluation on the work and prevent its score from quietly becoming the maker's worth.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a route to the visual or formal character selected for evaluation
semantic_domains.cognition: the assessed character or degree of something against criteria
semantic_domains.creation: the quality of a made or repaired result for its stated purpose
